impact: With P6's level error removed, residual shape error isolates to two bands at D1.00: 800Hz (plugin's twin-T notch stays ~11dB deeper than the pedal's -- the pedal's notch fills in at drive, the plugin's doesn't) and 3-4kHz (plugin +7.7dB; knob-tracking confirms pedal only gains +5.6dB from D0.50->D1.00 there vs plugin's +11.8dB, meaning the pedal is already saturated in that band at D0.50). Same class of problem as gap D (V2 zener drive tracking) -- both need drive-dependent saturation. Answer the GBW hypothesis from gap A/P6 first, since it may be the shared root cause for both this gap and gap D.
title: V1E drive-dependent band saturation (800Hz notch, 3-4kHz residual)
